clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Dietary intervention to prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with or without hypertension seeking prevention or treatment of elevated BP
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend DASH or heart-healthy eating pattern for BP management

navigationPath.purpose: Determine if patient has hypertension or elevated BP
Provide DASH meal plans and recipes

rules: If adult with OR without hypertension → eligible for DASH recommendation
COR 1 + LOE A → STRONG recommendation with high-quality evidence
Recommend DASH eating plan as primary dietary intervention
Goal: prevent elevated BP in normotensive adults OR treat hypertension in diagnosed adults
Provide specific guidance on sodium reduction, fruits/vegetables, whole grains, lean protein

categories.name: Level A
categories.source: >1 RCT or meta-analyses of high-quality RCTs

loeDescription.description: High-quality evidence from >1 RCT or meta-analyses of high-quality RCTs

recommendation.description: In adults with or without hypertension, a heart-healthy eating pattern, such as the DASH eating plan, is recommended to prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension

thresholds.sbp: N/A - dietary intervention not for acute crisis

appDisplay.screenType: Standard educational display with evidence badge

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Implement DASH eating plan with sodium reduction to <2300mg/day
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Reassess BP in 4 weeks, dietary adherence in 2 weeks
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP monthly; repeat dietary assessment at 3 months
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if patient unable to access recommended foods for social work referral
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log DASH recommendation to prevention/treatment plan; document patient education provided
